publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-12-30 23:18:06 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Blanchard, Kenneth H. (Title of work: Helping Cornell begin its journey towards legendary service..)

did.physdesc: Correspondence, memorabilia, photographs, printed materials, publications
did.abstract: Gligor Tashkovich was a Cornell graduate and Minister for Foreign Investment in the new Government in the Republic of Macedonia. The Gligor Tashkovich papers (1983-2015) contain under-graduate and post-graduate material including student and alumni activities, campus organizations and governance, personal correspondence and cards, US political and social life material, international committees and affairs, and items collected while traveling.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Cornell University Class of 1987, M.B.A. '91. In 2006, he was named Minister for Foreign Investment in the new Government in the Republic of Macedonia.

emph.ref: Series III. CORNELL UNIVERSITY: Under-graduate Materials.
Series IV. CORNELL UNIVERSITY: Post-graduate Materials.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Gligor Tashkovich papers, #41-05-3039. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Gligor Tashkovich papers (1983-2015) document Tashkovich's experience while a student at Cornell and his political and professional life. It contains under-graduate and post-graduate material including student and alumni activities, campus organizations and governance, personal correspondence and cards, US political and social life material, international committees and affairs, and items collected while traveling. Cornell organizations include Committee to Review the Dean of Students Office, Quill and Dagger, Seal & Serpent Society, the Public Safety Advisory Board, the Campus Store Administrative Board, and the Budget Policies Committee, and the Career Center of the Johnson Graduate School of Management. International affairs includes material from Macedonia, Albania, Azerbaijan, Bulgaria, Rumania, and other Balkan countries.

scopecontent.p: Document concerning a proposed first programmatic development policy loan of Euro 24.4 million to Macedonia. "For Official Use Only." Additional document titled "Key Sources of Growth" for Ohrid, Macedonia.

did.unittitle: ODIHR - Election Observation Mission: Bosnia and Herzegovina - Folder 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General Elections on October 3, 2010. ODIHR = Office for Democratic Insitutions and Human Rights. Contains a letter to Tashkovich explaining acceptance of position as a Short-Term Election Observer, along with an Interim Report from OSCE explaining the structure, concerns, and climate of the general elections. Included also is a compilation of legal documents for short-term observers, a contract for national staff, observation report forms, and other related materials.

did.unittitle: ODIHR - Election Observation Mission: Bosnia and Herzegovina - Folder 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuals for polling commissions in Bosnia and Herzegovina, a logistics information booklet, a blank qualitative election observation form, rules of conduct, and a regional briefing pack.

did.unittitle: ODIHR - Election Observation Mission: Bosnia and Herzegovina - Folder 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a glossary of election terminology, an election observation handbook, and an international observer guide.
